skill.name: creating-pull-requests
skill.description: Guides an AI assistant through checking a Git branch, pushing it, and creating a GitHub pull request with a structured description.
skill.summary: Create GitHub pull requests with branch checks, summaries, and test plans.
skill.author: 1gy

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are confirmed as intentional git and GitHub CLI examples for pull request creation. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown frontmatter and headings, with no cryptographic code present. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it can push branches and create remote GitHub pull requests.

medium_findings.title: Remote GitHub Commands Require User Review
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally allows and documents git and GitHub CLI commands, including pushing a branch and creating a pull request. This is appropriate for the stated purpose, but it can publish local work to a remote repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and semantically match the skill purpose. Risk remains medium because the operations affect a remote GitHub repository.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Pattern Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts point to Markdown fences, frontmatter text, and headings. No evidence found of Ruby execution, cryptographic functions, malware, prompt injection, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown metadata, headings, and code fences. The file contains no Ruby code or cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution In PR Body Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The PR creation example uses shell command substitution with a heredoc to pass a Markdown body to gh. The heredoc is locally constructed and not an exfiltration path, but shell quoting should be reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is visible and real, but it is used for a local PR body. The pattern is legitimate but deserves caution because shell syntax is involved.

content.user_title: Create Pull Requests With Clear Descriptions
content.value_statement: Pull request creation often misses branch checks, summaries, and test plans. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through GitHub PR preparation.
actual_capabilities: Checks the current Git branch before creating a pull request.
Reviews local status and commits that differ from the main branch.
Pushes a feature branch to the origin remote.
Creates a GitHub pull request with a title and structured body.
Supports common PR options such as draft, base branch, assignee, and label.
limitations: Requires Git and the GitHub CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Does not decide whether the code is correct or ready to merge.
Does not run tests unless the user requests them separately.
Can affect remote repositories, so users should confirm branch and base targets.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Create a pull request after checking commits, branch state, and the intended target branch.

use_cases.title: Standardize Review Requests
use_cases.description: Use a consistent summary and test plan format before asking teammates for review.

use_cases.title: Draft Work in Progress
use_cases.description: Open a draft pull request with clear context while implementation or testing continues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request for my current branch. Check the branch, status, and commits before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Draft Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a draft pull request for this branch with a concise summary and a checklist-based test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Target a Specific Base Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request from this branch into the release branch. Confirm the diff and include the test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Review-Ready PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the branch changes, create a polished pull request description, add labels if appropriate, and report the PR URL.

output_examples.input: Create a PR for the current feature branch.
output_examples.output: A pull request is created with a title, summary bullets, test plan checklist, target branch, and PR URL.

output_examples.input: Open a draft PR against the release branch.
output_examples.output: A draft pull request is opened against the requested base branch with the selected branch details and test notes.

output_examples.input: Prepare this branch for code review.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the branch status, summarizes commits, creates the PR, and returns the review link.

best_practices: Review the branch name, target branch, and diff before pushing.
Include a specific test plan that reviewers can verify.
Use draft pull requests when changes are not ready for final review.
anti_patterns: Do not create pull requests from a dirty working tree without explicit user approval.
Do not use vague summaries that hide the real behavior change.
Do not target the wrong base branch for hotfixes or release work.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides commands and structure for creating a GitHub pull request through Git and the GitHub CLI.

faq.question: Which tools does it require?
faq.answer: It requires Git and the GitHub CLI. The user must already be authenticated for repository access.

faq.question: Can it create draft pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents the draft option for work that is not ready for final review.

faq.question: Does it choose the correct base branch?
faq.answer: It prompts checking the project guidance and supports a base branch option. Users should confirm the target.

faq.question: Does it run tests before creating the PR?
faq.answer: No. It includes a test plan section, but test execution must be requested or performed separately.

faq.question: Is this safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It can be used in private repositories if access is configured. Users should review all remote operations first.
